title: Workflow Automation
description: To ensure consistent and efficient agent interactions across the SpecD lifecycle by defining policies for diagnostic output and data extraction. This spec minimizes "AI blindness" by mandating human-optimized text for status checks and machine-optimized formats for data-intensive operations.
context: To ensure consistent and efficient agent interactions across the SpecD lifecycle by defining policies for diagnostic output and data extraction. This spec minimizes "AI blindness" by mandating human-optimized text for status checks and machine-optimized formats for data-intensive operations.

rules: AI agents SHALL prioritize text-based output (`--format text`) for all lifecycle status checks, transition attempts, and validation commands to ensure visibility of human-readable blockers and notes.
AI agents SHALL use machine-optimized formats (`--format json`, `--format toon`) strictly when structured data extraction is required for subsequent tool calls or internal state management.
Agents SHALL follow the "Next Action" recommendations provided in command outputs before attempting to repeat a failed lifecycle operation.

scenarios.name: Agent chooses text format for status checks
when: an AI agent needs to check the current status of a change
then: it SHALL use specd change status <name> --format text
AND it MUST NOT use --format json as the primary diagnostic tool

scenarios.name: Agent chooses JSON for tool-call preparation
given: an agent needs to retrieve a list of spec IDs to pass to another tool
then: it SHALL use --format json or --format toon to ensure robust parsing of the spec ID array

scenarios.name: Agent follows repair guide after transition failure
given: a transition from designing to ready fails with MISSING_ARTIFACT
when: the CLI output provides a Repair Guide
then: the agent SHALL execute the recommended command (e.g., /specd-design)
AND it MUST NOT attempt to force the transition again without addressing the blocker
